user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and manage cataloger permissions, so that I can ensure accurate metadata records and efficient project execution.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of all collections in the repository, so that I can ensure projects are executed efficiently and on schedule.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of all collections in the repository, so that I can ensure projects are executed efficiently and on schedule.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of collections and view associated metadata profiles, so that I can effectively facilitate and oversee project execution.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of a collection, so that I can ensure the project stays on track and meets its goals.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to add catalogers to a collection, so that I can assign team members to help manage and describe the items efficiently.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items and their metadata profiles, so that I can ensure all required descriptive and structural metadata records are present and complete for each item in the repository.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to save a record without all of the required metadata so that I can start a record with the metadata I have and come back to finish it later.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be granted access to specific collections, so that I can create and maintain metadata records for items within those collections.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to be granted access to specific collections, so that I can view and update incomplete items within those collections.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to publish records only when the record has a Title, Identifier, Type, and

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create and edit metadata records for items in specific collections, so that the repository maintains accurate and up-to-date information.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want a list of all of the records I have access to so I can get to work quickly.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can ensure the quality and timely completion of projects.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to search for records based on status so I can track the progress of a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view all incomplete items in the repository, so that I can efficiently identify and complete missing metadata records.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to search for records based on all Duke Core fields.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and delete collections, so that I can organize items and manage repository content efficiently.
true_user_story: As an end user I want to be able to search across all collections at once.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and delete collections in the repository, so that I can effectively manage the content available to users.
true_user_story: As a cataloger and a collections manager I want Trident to support Duke Core so that metadata is consistent between collections.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and verify items using authority lists for metadata validation, so that I can ensure data quality and project execution.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want required DC fields to be enforced so that all records have values for all required DC fields. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create and update collections with associated metadata profiles and authority lists, so that items are consistently described and validated within the correct collection context.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want to be able to repeat repeatable DC Fields so that I can enter all of the relevant metadata. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in a collection, so that I can identify records that need further metadata editing and ensure repository data quality.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want Optional DC fields to be available but not required so that my collection can decide to use them or not. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in specific collections, so that I can create and maintain accurate metadata records according to the collection's metadata profile and authority lists.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to be able to create controlled vocabularies so that they can be used as authority lists during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in a collection, so that I can identify records that need further metadata editing or verification.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want authority lists to be easily accessible and contextually relevant so it is easy to use the authority.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in the repository, so that I can efficiently create and maintain accurate metadata records for items in my assigned collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to use external authority lists so I they can be used during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view and edit metadata records for media items in specific collections, so that I can ensure accurate and complete information is maintained and published.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read the digital object I am cataloging so I don't have to move between multiple applications while I'm creating metadata.

user_story: As a scanner operator, I want to create and update items and their components in the digital repository, so that scanned materials are accurately represented and managed.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read all components associated on the with an item on the create/update screen so I don't have to leave the form.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view and verify incomplete metadata records in specific collections, so that I can ensure the accuracy and completeness of the repository's data.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to see all of the metadata for a record while I'm editing it so that I have the full context for the item I'm cataloging.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can ensure the quality and timely completion of projects.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want each record to have a status so I can track the progress of the records in the collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress, so that I can ensure projects are executed efficiently and address any issues promptly.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want statuses to include a status that flags a record as having a problem so we can track issues as the arise.

user_story: As a Trident administrator, I want to assign specific permissions to different user roles, so that each user can access only the features and tasks relevant to their responsibilities.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want Trident to support the UTF-8 character set so that I can use any character necessary when creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to validate metadata fields against specified authority lists and ensure each item with components includes a structural metadata record, so that metadata is accurate and compliant with project requirements.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to map fields in inherited metadata to Duke Core fields and import a spreadsheet and create records.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to validate metadata fields against specified authority lists, so that I can ensure accurate and standardized descriptive metadata for each item in the project.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to import metadata and create records from a DCP digitazation guide.

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a repository user, I want to view and navigate bidirectional relationships between items (like images and finding aids), so that I can easily explore related content and understand how items are connected.
true_user_story: As a Archivist I want bi-directional linking between items in the digital collections and EAD finding aids

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a digital archivist, I want to create collections containing books, images, texts, and media, so that I can organize and relate different types of items for easier discovery and management.
true_user_story: As a End User I want to be able to see the physical context of an Item in a digital collection (i.e. See inside this folder; EAD)

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of a collection, so that I can ensure the project stays on track and meets its goals.
true_user_story: As a Collection Manager I want my collection to be discoverable in the Catalog

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to group components and check them for quality, so that the digitization process produces accurate and organized digital content.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU) I want to be able to create Components.

user_story: As a repository administrator, I want to add component files to a project in Trident and associate them with administrative and descriptive metadata records, so that each component is properly described, managed, and linked to a metadata profile for accurate organization and retrieval.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Technical Metadata with for Component.

user_story: As a repository administrator, I want to add component files to a Trident project with associated administrative and descriptive metadata, so that each component is properly described, managed, and linked to a metadata profile for accurate organization and retrieval.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Preservation Metadata for a Component.

user_story: As a digital archivist, I want to add component files to Trident and create administrative metadata, so that each atomic unit of digital content is properly cataloged and managed within the system.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to upload master surrogates to a Component.

user_story: As a repository manager, I want each item to require at least one descriptive metadata record and, if it has components, a structural metadata record, so that all items are discoverable and their structure is clearly defined.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to add one or more Components to an Item.

user_story: As a digital archivist, I want each item with components to include a structural metadata record and at least one descriptive metadata record, so that items are properly described and their structure is clearly documented for discovery and management.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create structural metadata for an Item so I can define the sequence of components.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to manage scanner operators and perform quality control assessments of scans, so that digitized materials are accurate and meet quality standards.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD) I want to group components that require digitization so that I can create "digitizable units".

user_story: As a scanner operator, I want to create, retrieve, update, and delete components for scanned items, so that I can ensure the digitized materials are accurate and complete.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to check-out/check-in groups of Components.

user_story: As a scanner operator, I want to check components for quality and correctness, so that only valid and accurate metadata is added to the system.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to verify the correctness and completeness of components so that I can ensure quality standards are met.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to assign scanner operators to specific collections, so that the digitization process is efficiently managed and operators are accountable for their assigned tasks.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to capture digitization statistics so that I can monitor Operator efficiency and make projections for future projects.

user_story: As a Trident administrator, I want to assign specific permissions to different user roles, so that each user can access only the features and tasks relevant to their responsibilities.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want Trident to create derivatives for me when I upload a Master.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can ensure project execution stays on track and issues are addressed promptly.
true_user_story: As a User I want to monitor the progress of a digital collection project.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to add and manage scanner operators in the system, so that I can efficiently assign digitization tasks and ensure proper workflow management.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been digitized.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to add scanner operators to the system and assign them to collections, so that I can efficiently manage who is responsible for digitizing specific items.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many digitized Items have been described.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items or collections, so that I can ensure the quality and timely completion of digital collections.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been published.♦

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor the progress of items (images, books, media, etc.) within a collection, so that I can efficiently execute and oversee project completion.
true_user_story: As Collection Manager or Sponsor I want to describe the collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want guidance on which authority lists to use for specific metadata fields, so that I can accurately validate and edit DMR records.
true_user_story: As a Preservation Librarian I want the relationship between masters and derivatives to be managed by the system so I don't have to.

user_story: As a collection manager, I want to assign a metadata profile to a collection, so that all items within the collection follow consistent metadata requirements.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define the metadata schema for a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to validate metadata fields against specified authority lists and profile requirements, so that I can ensure accurate and consistent metadata entry for items in a collection.
true_user_story: As a Metadata librarian I want to define Authorities to be used for specific fields in a collection MD Schema.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the system to validate descriptive metadata using authority lists and clear validation rules, so that I can ensure metadata accuracy and consistency when building or editing DMRs.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define validation rules for specific fields in a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the system to validate descriptive metadata using authority lists and clear validation rules, so that I can ensure metadata accuracy and consistency when building or editing DMRs.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want validation rules enforced when MD is submitted.
